title: noNonNullAssertedOptionalChain workaround path
rationale: When Biome's noNonNullAssertedOptionalChain blocks '?.' + '!' on values it can prove non-null, the right fix is explicit narrowing ('if (!x) throw') rather than 'as T' casts. 'as' silences the type system without runtime checks, defeating the purpose of removing '!' in the first place. Configuration (as of be40582): style.noNonNullAssertion is 'error' + --error-on-warnings, so future '!' regressions are prevented. Follow-up mulch-f92a reversed the test-cleanup 'as' workarounds from mulch-0775.

title: Baseline existing complexity violations via biome overrides
rationale: Enabling noExcessiveCognitiveComplexity (15) and noExcessiveLinesPerFunction (500) on a mature codebase triggers many violations in pre-existing code; refactoring 39+ files is out of scope for a scaffolding port. Per-file overrides keep the rules active for new code while lint stays green, matching the canopy L5 exemplar.

title: Mulch L5 ratchet scripts co-locate budgets in scripts/ and scan test/
rationale: Ported check-file-sizes.ts and check-debt-markers.ts from the L5 toolkit with paths/prefixes/budgets-only divergence (VAL-CROSS-001). Budget JSON lives in scripts/ (DEFAULT path resolves to scripts/*.json, not toolkit's ../budgets/), so verification/staging stays inside scripts/. Scan roots are ['src','test','scripts'] (toolkit uses ['src','scripts']) since mulch keeps tests under test/. Debt tracker prefixes are mulch-specific: '\\b(?:mulch|mx)-[0-9a-f]+\\b' plus #NNN and URL. Budgets baselined never tighter than observed: file-size threshold 500 with per-file ceilings = ceil(observed/50)*50+50; debt allowlist empty (0 untracked markers in tree). check:size and check:debt both exit 0.
